00020 
00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #if NEED_SCHED_H
00027 #   include "sched.h"
00028 #endif // NEED_SCHED_H
00029 
00030 #include "class.h"
00031 #include "interpreter.h"
00032 #include "kni.h"
00033 #include "loader.h"
00034 #include "memory.h"
00035 #include "thread.h"
00036 #include "vm.h"
00037 
00038 #include "java_lang_Thread.h"
00039 
00040 /******************************************************************************
00041  * Native thread wrapper function prototypes                                  *
00042  ******************************************************************************/
00043 
00044 static void native_key_create(native_key_t *);
00045 static void native_key_dispose(native_key_t);
00046 static void native_thread_create(native_thread_t *, void *(*start)(void *),
00047                                  void *);
00048 static void native_thread_exit( void );
00049 static void native_thread_yield( void );
00050 static void native_mutex_create(native_mutex_t *);
00051 static void native_mutex_dispose(native_mutex_t *);
00052 static void native_mutex_lock(native_mutex_t *);
00053 static void native_mutex_unlock(native_mutex_t *);
00054 static void native_cond_create(native_cond_t *);
00055 static void native_cond_dispose(native_cond_t *);
00056 static void native_cond_wait(native_cond_t *, native_mutex_t *);
00057 static void native_cond_timed_wait(native_cond_t *, native_mutex_t *,
00058                                    uint64_t, uint32_t);
00059 static void native_cond_signal(native_cond_t *);
00060 static void native_cond_broadcast(native_cond_t *);
00061 
00062 /******************************************************************************
00063  * Native thread wrapper functions                                            *
00064  ******************************************************************************/
00065 
00069 static void native_key_create(native_key_t *key)
00070 {
00071 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX && !defined(TLS)
00072     pthread_key_create(key, NULL);
00073 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00074     pth_key_create(key, NULL);
00075 #endif
00076 } // native_key_create()
00077 
00081 static void native_key_dispose(native_key_t key)
00082 {
00083 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX && !defined(TLS)
00084     pthread_key_delete(key);
00085 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00086     pth_key_delete(key);
00087 #endif
00088 } // native_key_dispose()
00089 
00095 static void native_thread_create(native_thread_t *thread,
00096                                  void *(*start)(void *), void *arg)
00097 {
00098 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00099     int res;
00100     pthread_attr_t attr;
00101 
00102     pthread_attr_init(&attr);
00103     pthread_attr_setdetachstate(&attr, PTHREAD_CREATE_DETACHED);
00104 
00105     res = pthread_create(thread, &attr, start, arg);
00106 
00107     if (res) {
00108         dbg_error("Unable to create a new thread");
00109         vm_fail();
00110     }
00111 
00112     pthread_attr_destroy(&attr);
00113 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00114     pth_attr_t attr;
00115 
00116     attr = pth_attr_new();
00117     pth_attr_set(attr, PTH_ATTR_JOINABLE, FALSE);
00118 
00119     *thread = pth_spawn(attr, start, arg);
00120 
00121     if (*thread == NULL) {
00122         dbg_error("Unable to create a new thread");
00123         vm_fail();
00124     }
00125 
00126     pth_attr_destroy(attr);
00127 #endif
00128 } // native_thread_create()
00129 
00132 static void native_thread_exit( void )
00133 {
00134 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00135     pthread_exit(NULL);
00136 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00137     pth_exit(NULL);
00138 #endif
00139 } // native_thread_exit()
00140 
00143 static void native_thread_yield( void )
00144 {
00145 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00146 #   if HAVE_PTHREAD_YIELD_NP
00147     pthread_yield_np();
00148 #   elif HAVE_PTHREAD_YIELD
00149     pthread_yield();
00150 #   elif HAVE_SCHED_YIELD
00151     ; // NOP
00152 #   endif // JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00153 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00154     pth_yield(NULL);
00155 #endif
00156 } // native_thread_yield()
00157 
00161 static void native_mutex_create(native_mutex_t *mutex)
00162 {
00163 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00164     pthread_mutexattr_t attr;
00165 
00166     pthread_mutexattr_init(&attr);
00167     pthread_mutexattr_settype(&attr, PTHREAD_MUTEX_RECURSIVE);
00168     pthread_mutex_init(mutex, &attr);
00169     pthread_mutexattr_destroy(&attr);
00170 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00171     pth_mutex_init(mutex);
00172 #endif
00173 } // native_mutex_create()
00174 
00178 static void native_mutex_dispose(native_mutex_t *mutex)
00179 {
00180 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00181     pthread_mutex_destroy(mutex);
00182 #endif // JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00183 } // native_mutex_dispose()
00184 
00188 static void native_mutex_lock(native_mutex_t *mutex)
00189 {
00190 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00191     pthread_mutex_lock(mutex);
00192 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00193     pth_mutex_acquire(mutex, FALSE, NULL);
00194 #endif
00195 } // native_mutex_lock()
00196 
00200 static void native_mutex_unlock(native_mutex_t *mutex)
00201 {
00202 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00203     pthread_mutex_unlock(mutex);
00204 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00205     pth_mutex_release(mutex);
00206  #endif
00207 } // native_mutex_unlock()
00208 
00212 static void native_cond_create(native_cond_t *cond)
00213 {
00214 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00215     pthread_cond_init(cond, NULL);
00216 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00217     pth_cond_init(cond);
00218 #endif
00219 } // native_cond_dispose()
00220 
00224 static void native_cond_dispose(native_cond_t *cond)
00225 {
00226 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00227     while (pthread_cond_destroy(cond) != 0) {
00228         ;
00229     }
00230 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00231     /* HACK, GROSS: This relies on internal information of GNU/Pth, we should
00232      * find a better way for checking that there are no more waiters */
00233     while (cond->cn_waiters != 0) {
00234         pth_yield(NULL);
00235     }
00236 #endif
00237 } // native_cond_dispose()
00238 
00244 static void native_cond_wait(native_cond_t *cond, native_mutex_t *mutex)
00245 {
00246 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00247     pthread_cond_wait(cond, mutex);
00248 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00249     pth_cond_await(cond, mutex, NULL);
00250 #endif
00251 } // native_cond_wait()
00252 
00260 static void native_cond_timed_wait(native_cond_t *cond, native_mutex_t *mutex,
00261                                    uint64_t millis, uint32_t nanos)
00262 {
00263 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00264     if ((millis == 0) && (nanos == 0)) {
00265         pthread_cond_wait(cond, mutex);
00266     } else {
00267         struct timespec req = get_time_with_offset(millis, nanos);
00268 
00269         pthread_cond_timedwait(cond, mutex, &req);
00270     }
00271 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00272     if ((millis == 0) && (nanos == 0)) {
00273           pth_cond_await(cond, mutex, NULL);
00274     } else {
00275         pth_event_t ev;
00276 
00277         ev = pth_event(PTH_EVENT_TIME,
00278                        pth_timeout(millis / 1000,
00279                                    (millis % 1000) * 1000000 + nanos));
00280         pth_cond_await(cond, mutex, ev);
00281         pth_event_free(ev, PTH_FREE_ALL);
00282     }
00283 #else
00284 struct timespec req2;
00285     struct timespec req = {
00286         millis / 1000,
00287         (millis % 1000) * 1000000 + nanos
00288     };
00289 
00290     // Threading is disabled
00291     nanosleep(&req, NULL);
00292 #endif
00293 } // native_cond_timed_wait()
00294 
00298 static void native_cond_signal(native_cond_t *cond)
00299 {
00300 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00301     pthread_cond_signal(cond);
00302 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00303     pth_cond_notify(cond, false);
00304 #endif
00305 } // native_cond_signal()
00306 
00310 static void native_cond_broadcast(native_cond_t *cond)
00311 {
00312 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00313     pthread_cond_broadcast(cond);
00314 #elif JEL_THREAD_PTH
00315     pth_cond_notify(cond, true);
00316 #endif
00317 } // native_cond_broadcast()
00318 
00319 /******************************************************************************
00320  * Thread manager                                                             *
00321  ******************************************************************************/
00322 
00326 struct thread_payload_t {
00327     method_t *run; 
00328     uintptr_t *ref; 
00329     native_thread_t thread; 
00330     native_cond_t cond; 
00331 };
00332 
00334 typedef struct thread_payload_t thread_payload_t;
00335 
00338 struct monitor_t {
00339     struct monitor_t *next; 
00340     uintptr_t ref; 
00341     thread_t *owner; 
00342     size_t count; 
00343     native_cond_t *cond; 
00344 };
00345 
00347 typedef struct monitor_t monitor_t;
00348 
00350 #define TM_CAPACITY (4)
00351 
00355 struct thread_manager_t {
00356     native_mutex_t lock; 
00357 #if JEL_THREAD_PTH
00358     size_t switch_n; 
00359 #endif // JEL_THREAD_PTH
00360     size_t active; 
00361     thread_t *queue; 
00362     size_t capacity; 
00363     size_t entries; 
00364     monitor_t *buckets; 
00365 };
00366 
00368 typedef struct thread_manager_t thread_manager_t;
00369 
00370 /******************************************************************************
00371  * Globals                                                                    *
00372  ******************************************************************************/
00373 
00375 JEL_TLS native_key_t self;
00376 
00378 static thread_manager_t tm;
00379 
00380 /******************************************************************************
00381  * Thread manager implementation                                              *
00382  ******************************************************************************/
00383 
00386 #define PTH_SWITCH_N (500)
00387 
00392 void tm_init( void )
00393 {
00394     memset(&tm, 0, sizeof(thread_manager_t));
00395 
00396 #if JEL_THREAD_PTH
00397     if (!pth_init()) {
00398         vm_fail();
00399     }
00400 
00401     tm.switch_n = PTH_SWITCH_N;
00402 #endif // JEL_THREAD_PTH
00403 
00404     /* When using multi-threading the global machine lock is recursive so that
00405      * the code can enter more than one syncrhonized sections safely */
00406     native_mutex_create(&tm.lock);
00407     native_key_create(&self);
00408 } // tm_init()
00409 
00412 void tm_teardown( void )
00413 {
00414 #if JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00415     thread_t *thread;
00416 
00417     /* FIXME: We should wait for all threads to complete instead of canceling
00418      * them */
00419 
00420     tm_lock();
00421     tm_stop_the_world();
00422 
00423     for (thread = tm.queue; thread != NULL; thread = thread->next) {
00424         pthread_cancel(thread->native);
00425     }
00426 
00427     tm_unlock();
00428 #endif // JEL_THREAD_POSIX
00429 
00430     native_key_dispose(self);
00431     native_mutex_dispose(&tm.lock);
00432 
00433 #if JEL_THREAD_PTH
00434     pth_kill();
00435 #endif // JEL_THREAD_PTH
00436 } // tm_teardown()
00437 
00441 void tm_register(thread_t *thread)
00442 {
00443     thread->next = tm.queue;
00444     tm.queue = thread;
00445     tm.active++;
00446 } // tm_register()
00447 
00451 void tm_unregister(thread_t *thread)
00452 {
00453     thread_t *prev, *curr;
00454 
00455     prev = NULL;
00456     curr = tm.queue;
00457 
00458     while (thread != curr) {
00459         prev = curr;
00460         curr = curr->next;
00461     }
00462 
00463     if (prev == NULL) {
00464         tm.queue = curr->next;
00465     } else {
00466         prev->next = curr->next;
00467     }
00468 
00469     tm.active--;
00470 } // tm_unregister_thread()
00471 
00475 uint32_t tm_active( void )
00476 {
00477 #if JEL_FINALIZER
00478     return tm.active - 1;
00479 #else
00480     return tm.active;
00481 #endif // JEL_FINALIZER
00482 } // tm_active()
00483 
00486 void tm_mark( void )
00487 {
00488     jword_t *scan;
00489     thread_t *thread;
00490 
00491     for (thread = tm.queue; thread != NULL; thread = thread->next) {
00492         gc_mark_reference(thread->obj);
00493 
00494         // Mark the temporary roots
00495         for (size_t i = 0; i < thread->roots.used; i++) {
00496             gc_mark_potential(*(thread->roots.pointers[i]));
00497         }
00498 
00499         // Crawl the stack
00500         if (thread->stack != NULL) {
00501             for (scan = thread->stack; scan < thread->sp; scan++) {
00502                 gc_mark_potential(*((uintptr_t *) scan));
00503             }
00504         }
00505 
00506         // Mark the pending exception
00507         gc_mark_reference(thread->exception);
00508     }
00509 } // tm_mark()
00510 
00514 static size_t tm_hash(uintptr_t ref)
00515 {
00516 #if SIZEOF_VOID_P == 4
00517     return (ref >> 2);
00518 #else
00519     return (ref >> 3);
00520 #endif
00521 } // tm_hash()
00522 
00526 void tm_purge( void )
00527 {
00528     size_t hash, entries = 0;
00529     monitor_t *entry, *other;
00530     header_t *header;
00531 
00532     for (size_t i = 0; i < tm.capacity; i++) {
00533         entry = tm.buckets + i;
00534 
00535         if (entry->ref) {
00536             header = (header_t *) entry->ref;
00537 
00538             if (!header_is_marked(header)) {
00539                 /* The object referenced by this monitor is dead, let's purge
00540                  * the monitor then */
00541 
00542                 if (entry->cond) {
00543                     native_cond_dispose(entry->cond);
00544                     gc_free(entry->cond);
00545                 }
00546 
00547                 memset(entry, 0, sizeof(monitor_t));
00548             } else {
00549                 entries++;
00550             }
00551         }
00552     }
00553 
00554     tm.entries = entries;
00555 
00556     /* This first phase will scan the buckets looking for the remaining
00557      * monitors. If a monitor is not in the bucket corresponding to its hash
00558      * value we check the bucket corresponding to the hash value. If it is
00559      * empty the monitor is moved there, if it is full but the monitor there
00560      * does not correspond to the hash value the two monitors are swapped.
00561      * This phase also clears the 'next' field of the monitors so that we can
00562      * re-chain them in the next phase */
00563 
00564     for (size_t i = 0; i < tm.capacity; i++) {
00565         entry = tm.buckets + i;
00566 
00567         if (entry->ref) {
00568             entry->next = NULL;
00569             hash = tm_hash(entry->ref) & (tm.capacity - 1);
00570 
00571             if (i != hash) {
00572                 other = tm.buckets + hash;
00573 
00574                 if (other->ref == JNULL) {
00575                     *other = *entry;
00576                     memset(entry, 0, sizeof(monitor_t));
00577                 } else if ((tm_hash(other->ref) & (tm.capacity - 1)) != hash) {
00578                     monitor_t dummy = *other;
00579                     *other = *entry;
00580                     *entry = dummy;
00581                 }
00582             }
00583         }
00584     }
00585 
00586     /* We look for the entries that are in the buckets which are not associated
00587      * with their hash value. Those entries are chained after the entries in
00588      * the buckets associated with their hash value */
00589 
00590     for (size_t i = 0; i < tm.capacity; i++) {
00591         entry = tm.buckets + i;
00592 
00593         if (entry->ref) {
00594             hash = tm_hash(entry->ref) & (tm.capacity - 1);
00595 
00596             if (i != hash) {
00597                 entry->next = tm.buckets[hash].next;
00598                 tm.buckets[hash].next = entry;
00599             }
00600         }
00601     }
00602 } // tm_purge()
00603 
00607 static void tm_rehash(bool grow)
00608 {
00609     size_t capacity, hash;
00610     size_t i, j;
00611     monitor_t *buckets, *entry;
00612 
00613     if (grow) {
00614         capacity = tm.capacity * 2;
00615     } else {
00616         capacity = tm.capacity / 2;
00617     }
00618 
00619     buckets = gc_malloc(capacity * sizeof(monitor_t));
00620 
00621     for (i = 0; i < tm.capacity; i++) {
00622         entry = tm.buckets + i;
00623 
00624         if (entry->ref) {
00625             j = hash = tm_hash(entry->ref) & (capacity - 1);
00626 
00627             while (buckets[j].ref != JNULL) {
00628                 j = (j + 1) & (capacity - 1);
00629             }
00630 
00631             buckets[j].ref = entry->ref;
00632             buckets[j].owner = entry->owner;
00633             buckets[j].count = entry->count;
00634             buckets[j].cond = entry->cond;
00635 
00636             // Chain the entry if there was a clash
00637             if (j == hash) {
00638                 buckets[j].next = NULL;
00639             } else {
00640                 buckets[j].next = buckets[hash].next;
00641                 buckets[hash].next = buckets + j;
00642             }
00643         }
00644     }
00645 
00646     gc_free(tm.buckets);
00647     tm.capacity = capacity;
00648     tm.buckets = buckets;
00649 } // tm_rehash()
00650 
00651 #if !JEL_THREAD_NONE
00652 
00659 void tm_lock( void )
00660 {
00661     thread_may_block();
00662     native_mutex_lock(&tm.lock);
00663     thread_resumes();
00664 } // tm_lock()
00665 
00668 void tm_unlock( void )
00669 {
00670     native_mutex_unlock(&tm.lock);
00671 
00672 #if JEL_THREAD_PTH
00673     if (--tm.switch_n == 0) {
00674         tm.switch_n = PTH_SWITCH_N;
00675         pth_yield(NULL);
00676     }
00677 #endif // JEL_THREAD_PTH
00678 } // tm_unlock()
00679 
00683 void tm_stop_the_world( void )
00684 {
00685     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00686     thread_t *thread;
00687     bool stopped = false;
00688 
00689     // Inform all threads that they must stop
00690     self->blocked = true;
00691 
00692     for (thread = tm.queue; thread != NULL; thread = thread->next) {
00693         thread->stopped = true;
00694     }
00695 
00696     // Wait for all the threads to stop
00697     while (!stopped) {
00698         stopped = true;
00699 
00700         for (thread = tm.queue; thread != NULL; thread = thread->next) {
00701             stopped &= thread->blocked;
00702         }
00703 
00704         thread_yield();
00705     }
00706 
00707     self->blocked = false;
00708 } // tm_stop_the_world()
00709 
00710 #endif // !JEL_THREAD_NONE
00711 
00712 /******************************************************************************
00713  * Monitors                                                                   *
00714  ******************************************************************************/
00715 
00718 void monitor_init( void )
00719 {
00720     tm.capacity = TM_CAPACITY;
00721     tm.buckets = gc_malloc(TM_CAPACITY * sizeof(monitor_t));
00722 } // monitor_init()
00723 
00728 void monitor_enter(thread_t *thread, uintptr_t ref)
00729 {
00730     monitor_t *entry;
00731     size_t hash;
00732     bool done;
00733 
00734     do {
00735         done = true;
00736         tm_lock();
00737 
00738         hash = tm_hash(ref) & (tm.capacity - 1);
00739 
00740         // Check if the monitor is already present
00741         entry = tm.buckets + hash;
00742 
00743         while (entry && (entry->ref != ref)) {
00744             entry = entry->next;
00745         }
00746 
00747         if (entry) {
00748             // The monitor is already present
00749             if (entry->owner == NULL) {
00750                 entry->owner = thread;
00751                 entry->count = 1;
00752             } else if (entry->owner == thread) {
00753                 entry->count++;
00754             } else {
00755                 // This monitor is contended, spin
00756                 tm_unlock();
00757                 thread_yield();
00758                 done = false;
00759             }
00760         } else {
00761             // The monitor is not there, let's insert a new one
00762             size_t i = hash;
00763 
00764             while (tm.buckets[i].ref != JNULL) {
00765                 i = (i + 1) & (tm.capacity - 1);
00766             }
00767 
00768             entry = tm.buckets + i;
00769             entry->ref = ref;
00770             entry->owner = thread;
00771             entry->count = 1;
00772 
00773             // Chain the entry if there was a clash
00774             if (i == hash) {
00775                 entry->next = NULL;
00776             } else {
00777                 entry->next = tm.buckets[hash].next;
00778                 tm.buckets[hash].next = entry;
00779             }
00780 
00781             // Check if we must grow the table
00782             tm.entries++;
00783 
00784             if (tm.entries == tm.capacity) {
00785                 tm_rehash(true);
00786             } else if (tm.entries < (tm.capacity / 4)) {
00787                 tm_rehash(false);
00788             }
00789         }
00790     } while (!done);
00791 
00792     tm_unlock();
00793 } // monitor_enter()
00794 
00801 bool monitor_exit(thread_t *thread, uintptr_t ref)
00802 {
00803     monitor_t *entry;
00804     size_t hash;
00805     bool done = false;
00806 
00807     tm_lock();
00808     hash = tm_hash(ref) & (tm.capacity - 1);
00809 
00810     // Look for the monitor
00811     entry = tm.buckets + hash;
00812 
00813     while (entry && (entry->ref != ref)) {
00814         entry = entry->next;
00815     }
00816 
00817     if (entry) {
00818         if (entry->owner == thread) {
00819             if (entry->count == 1) {
00820                 entry->owner = NULL;
00821             }
00822 
00823             entry->count--;
00824             done = true;
00825         }
00826     }
00827 
00828     tm_unlock();
00829 
00830     return done;
00831 } // monitor_exit()
00832 
00833 /******************************************************************************
00834  * Thread implementation                                                      *
00835  ******************************************************************************/
00836 
00840 static void thread_set_self(thread_t *thread)
00841 {
00842     native_key_set(&self, thread);
00843 } // thread_set_self()
00844 
00849 void thread_push_root(uintptr_t *ref)
00850 {
00851     thread_t *thread = thread_self();
00852     uintptr_t **pointers;
00853 
00854     thread->roots.pointers[thread->roots.used++] = ref;
00855 
00856     /* Grow the stack so that a free slot is always available when entering
00857      * this function */
00858     if (thread->roots.used == thread->roots.capacity) {
00859         pointers = gc_malloc((thread->roots.capacity + 1)
00860                              * sizeof(uintptr_t *));
00861         memcpy(pointers, thread->roots.pointers,
00862                thread->roots.capacity * sizeof(uintptr_t *));
00863         gc_free(thread->roots.pointers);
00864         thread->roots.pointers = pointers;
00865         thread->roots.capacity++;
00866     }
00867 } // thread_push_root()
00868 
00871 void thread_pop_root( void )
00872 {
00873     thread_self()->roots.used--;
00874 } // thread_pop_root()
00875 
00880 void thread_init(thread_t *thread)
00881 {
00882     memset(thread, 0, sizeof(thread_t));
00883     thread_set_self(thread);
00884     native_cond_create(&thread->cond);
00885 } // thread_init()
00886 
00896 uintptr_t thread_create_main(thread_t *thread, method_t *run, uintptr_t *args)
00897 {
00898     class_t *thread_cl = bcl_find_class("java/lang/Thread");
00899     size_t stack_size = opts_get_stack_size();
00900 
00901     /* Contrary to the startup of a 'regular' thread we do not initialize nor
00902      * register the thread as the main thread has already been initialized and
00903      * registered even though it is only partially initialized */
00904 
00905     // Build the stack
00906     thread->stack = gc_malloc(stack_size);
00907     thread->sp = thread->stack;
00908     thread->fp = (stack_frame_t *) ((char *) thread->stack + stack_size);
00909 
00910     /* Create the first Java thread structure, since the first thread is not
00911      * created by VMThread.start() we have to do it by hand */
00912     thread->obj = gc_new(thread_cl);
00913     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread->obj)->vmThread = (uintptr_t) thread;
00914     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread->obj)->priority = 5;
00915     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread->obj)->name =
00916         JAVA_LANG_STRING_PTR2REF(jstring_create_from_utf8("Thread-0"));
00917 
00918     // HACK: Push the arguments on top of the stack
00919     *((uintptr_t *) thread->sp) = *args;
00920 
00921     // Run the new thread's code
00922     interpreter(run);
00923 
00924     // Mark the thread as dead, none can join on it anymore after this point
00925     tm_lock();
00926     native_cond_broadcast(&thread->cond);
00927     tm_unregister(thread);
00928     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread->obj)->vmThread = JNULL;
00929     tm_unlock();
00930 
00931     gc_free(thread->roots.pointers);
00932     gc_free(thread->stack);
00933 
00934     return thread->exception;
00935 } // thread_create_main()
00936 
00941 void thread_sleep(int64_t ms)
00942 {
00943     thread_t *self = thread_self();
00944 
00945     tm_lock();
00946 
00947     if (!self->interrupted) {
00948         native_cond_t cond; // Dummy condition variable
00949 
00950         native_cond_create(&cond);
00951         self->cond_int = &cond;
00952         thread_may_block();
00953         native_cond_timed_wait(&cond, &tm.lock, ms, 0);
00954         thread_resumes();
00955         self->cond_int = NULL;
00956     }
00957 
00958     if (self->interrupted) {
00959         self->interrupted = false;
00960         KNI_ThrowNew("java/lang/InterruptedException", NULL);
00961     }
00962 
00963     tm_unlock();
00964 } // thread_sleep()
00965 
00966 #if !JEL_THREAD_NONE
00967 
00974 static void *thread_start(void *arg)
00975 {
00976     size_t stack_size = opts_get_stack_size();
00977     thread_payload_t *payload = (thread_payload_t *) arg;
00978     method_t *run = payload->run;
00979     uintptr_t *ref = payload->ref;
00980     thread_t thread;
00981     int exception;
00982 
00983     // Initialize the new thread
00984     thread_init(&thread);
00985 
00986     // Build the stack
00987     thread.stack = gc_malloc(stack_size);
00988     thread.sp = thread.stack;
00989     thread.fp = (stack_frame_t *) ((char *) thread.stack + stack_size);
00990 
00991     // Create the temporary roots area
00992     thread.roots.capacity = THREAD_TMP_ROOTS;
00993     thread.roots.pointers = gc_malloc(THREAD_TMP_ROOTS * sizeof(uintptr_t *));
00994 
00995     tm_lock();
00996 
00997     // Link the java.lang.Thread object to the VM thread and register it
00998     thread.obj = *ref;
00999     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread.obj)->vmThread = (uintptr_t) &thread;
01000     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread.obj)->priority = 5;
01001     tm_register(&thread);
01002 
01003     // Inform the parent thread that we're starting execution
01004     thread.native = payload->thread;
01005     native_cond_signal(&payload->cond);
01006     tm_unlock();
01007 
01008     // HACK: Push the 'this' pointer on top of the stack
01009     *((uintptr_t *) thread.stack) = thread.obj;
01010 
01011     c_try {
01012         interpreter(run); // Run the new thread's code
01013     } c_catch (exception) {
01014         c_print_exception(exception);
01015         c_clear_exception();
01016         /* FIXME: The thread must shut down but the rest of the VM should be
01017          * kept running and joining threads must be woken up. */
01018         vm_fail();
01019     }
01020 
01021     // Mark the thread as dead, none can join on it anymore after this point
01022     tm_lock();
01023 
01024     native_cond_broadcast(&thread.cond);
01025     tm_unregister(&thread);
01026     JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(thread.obj)->vmThread = JNULL;
01027 
01028     /* We can safely release the stack and root pointers now that the thread
01029      * is not visible anymore to the garbage collector */
01030     gc_free(thread.roots.pointers);
01031     gc_free(thread.stack);
01032 
01033     tm_unlock();
01034 
01035     // Wait for the joining thread to wake up
01036     native_cond_dispose(&thread.cond);
01037 
01038     if (thread.exception != JNULL) {
01039         // TODO: Print the exception and stack trace
01040         dbg_error("Uncaught exception");
01041         vm_fail();
01042     }
01043 
01044     native_thread_exit();
01045 
01046     return NULL;
01047 } // thread_start()
01048 
01053 void thread_launch(uintptr_t *ref, method_t *run)
01054 {
01055     thread_payload_t *payload;
01056 
01057     // Prepare the thread payload
01058     payload = gc_malloc(sizeof(thread_payload_t));
01059     payload->run = run;
01060     payload->ref = ref;
01061     native_cond_create(&payload->cond);
01062 
01063     // We take the global lock for we will be waiting for the new thread
01064     tm_lock();
01065 
01066     // FIXME: Check if thread creation fails and shut down the machine 'cleanly'
01067     native_thread_create(&payload->thread, thread_start, payload);
01068 
01069     // Wait for the new thread
01070     thread_may_block();
01071     native_cond_wait(&payload->cond, &tm.lock);
01072     thread_resumes();
01073     tm_unlock();
01074 
01075     // Cleanup
01076     native_cond_dispose(&payload->cond);
01077     gc_free(payload);
01078 } // thread_launch()
01079 
01086 void thread_may_block( void )
01087 {
01088     thread_t *self = thread_self();
01089 
01090     assert(!self->blocked);
01091     self->blocked = true;
01092 } // thread_may_block()
01093 
01100 void thread_resumes( void )
01101 {
01102     thread_t *self = thread_self();
01103 
01104     assert(self->blocked);
01105     self->blocked = false;
01106 
01107     // Check if a stop-the world has been requested
01108     if (self->stopped) {
01109         /* Mark the thread as blocked again so that the tm_stop_the_world() can
01110          * make sure that this thread is effectively blocked */
01111         self->blocked = true;
01112 
01113         /* The global lock is already held by the thread that called
01114          * tm_stop_the_world() so this thread will block until the lock has
01115          * been released */
01116         native_mutex_lock(&tm.lock);
01117         native_mutex_unlock(&tm.lock);
01118 
01119         self->stopped = false;
01120         self->blocked = false;
01121     }
01122 } // thread_resumes()
01123 
01130 void thread_interrupt(thread_t *thread)
01131 {
01132     tm_lock();
01133     thread->interrupted = true;
01134 
01135     if (thread->cond_int) {
01136         native_cond_signal(thread->cond_int);
01137     }
01138 
01139     tm_unlock();
01140 } // thread_interrupt()
01141 
01145 void thread_yield( void )
01146 {
01147     native_thread_yield();
01148 } // thread_yield()
01149 
01155 void thread_join(uintptr_t *thread)
01156 {
01157     thread_t *self = thread_self();
01158     thread_t *target;
01159 
01160     tm_lock();
01161 
01162     if (!self->interrupted) {
01163         target = (thread_t *) JAVA_LANG_THREAD_REF2PTR(*thread)->vmThread;
01164 
01165         if (target != NULL) {
01166             self->cond_int = &target->cond;
01167             thread_may_block();
01168             native_cond_wait(&target->cond, &tm.lock);
01169             thread_resumes();
01170             self->cond_int = NULL;
01171         }
01172     }
01173 
01174     if (self->interrupted) {
01175         self->interrupted = false;
01176         KNI_ThrowNew("java/lang/InterruptedException", NULL);
01177     }
01178 
01179     tm_unlock();
01180 } // thread_join()
01181 
01192 bool thread_wait(uintptr_t ref, uint64_t millis, uint32_t nanos)
01193 {
01194     thread_t *self = thread_self();
01195     monitor_t *entry;
01196     size_t hash;
01197 
01198     tm_lock();
01199 
01200     // Look for the monitor
01201     hash = tm_hash(ref) & (tm.capacity - 1);
01202     entry = tm.buckets + hash;
01203 
01204     while (entry && (entry->ref != ref)) {
01205         entry = entry->next;
01206     }
01207 
01208     if (entry && !self->interrupted) {
01209         if ((entry->owner == self) && (entry->count == 1)) {
01210             // Release the lock and wait
01211             entry->owner = NULL;
01212             entry->count = 0;
01213 
01214             if (entry->cond == NULL) {
01215                 entry->cond = gc_malloc(sizeof(native_cond_t));
01216                 native_cond_create(entry->cond);
01217             }
01218 
01219             self->cond_int = entry->cond;
01220             thread_may_block();
01221 
01222             if ((millis == 0) && (nanos == 0)) {
01223                 native_cond_wait(entry->cond, &tm.lock);
01224             } else {
01225                 native_cond_timed_wait(entry->cond, &tm.lock, millis, nanos);
01226             }
01227 
01228             thread_resumes();
01229             self->cond_int = NULL;
01230 
01231             // Re-acquire the monitor
01232             tm_unlock();
01233             monitor_enter(self, ref);
01234         }
01235     } else {
01236         tm_unlock();
01237     }
01238 
01239     if (self->interrupted) {
01240         self->interrupted = false;
01241         KNI_ThrowNew("java/lang/InterruptedException", NULL);
01242     }
01243 
01244     return entry ? true : false;
01245 } // thread_wait()
01246 
01255 bool thread_notify(uintptr_t ref, bool broadcast)
01256 {
01257     thread_t *self = thread_self();
01258     monitor_t *entry;
01259     size_t hash;
01260     bool res = false;
01261 
01262     tm_lock();
01263 
01264     // Look for the monitor
01265     hash = tm_hash(ref) & (tm.capacity - 1);
01266     entry = tm.buckets + hash;
01267 
01268     while (entry && (entry->ref != ref)) {
01269         entry = entry->next;
01270     }
01271 
01272     if (entry) {
01273         if (entry->owner == self) {
01274             // Notify the waiting thread
01275             if (entry->cond != NULL) {
01276                 if (broadcast) {
01277                     native_cond_broadcast(entry->cond);
01278                 } else {
01279                     native_cond_signal(entry->cond);
01280                 }
01281             }
01282 
01283             res = true;
01284         }
01285     }
01286 
01287     tm_unlock();
01288     return res;
01289 } // thread_notify()
01290 
01291 #endif // JEL_THREAD_POSIX || JEL_THREAD_PTH
01292
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:38:46 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
